# Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

**Jagti, NH-44, Nagrota, Jammu (Jammu & Kashmir) – 181221**
**E-mail:** registrar@iitjammu.ac.in

---

**Reference No.:** IITJMU/SW/11-17/2/2025/
**Date:** 23 July 2025

# NOTIFICATION

## Subject: Appointment of Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS) for Upcoming Semester

The Competent Authority has approved the appointment of the following **Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS)** for the upcoming semester.

## Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS) Schedule

| S. No. | Faculty 1                  | Faculty 2                | Date        |
| ------ | -------------------------- | ------------------------ | ----------- |
| 1      | Dr. Ravi Kumar Arun        | Dr. Abhishek Ghosh       | 27-Jul-2025 |
| 2      | Dr. Ashok Bera             | Dr. Sarah Mariam Abraham | 28-Jul-2025 |
| 3      | Dr. Ajeet Kumar Sharma     | Dr. Abhishek Kumar       | 29-Jul-2025 |
| 4      | Dr. Vijay Kumar Pal        | Dr. Riya Bhowmik         | 30-Jul-2025 |
| 5      | Dr. Sumit Kumar Pandey     | Dr. Padmini Singh        | 31-Jul-2025 |
| 6      | Dr. Ankit Tyagi            | Dr. Suman Banerjee       | 01-Aug-2025 |
| 7      | Dr. Devi Lal               | Dr. Akash Subhash Awale  | 02-Aug-2025 |
| 8      | Dr. Ashutosh Yadav         | Dr. Rani Rohini          | 03-Aug-2025 |
| 9      | Dr. Shiva Sekar            | Dr. Chandan Yadav        | 04-Aug-2025 |
| 10     | Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah     | Dr. Sukanya Mondal       | 05-Aug-2025 |
| 11     | Dr. Samresh Bera           | Dr. Dhanendra Kumar      | 06-Aug-2025 |
| 12     | Dr. Nalin Kumar Sharma     | Dr. Garima Singh         | 07-Aug-2025 |
| 13     | Dr. Roshan Udaram Patil    | Dr. Hardeep Singh        | 08-Aug-2025 |
| 14     | Dr. Venkata Sathish Akella | Dr. Kishore Kumar Jagini | 09-Aug-2025 |
| 15     | Dr. Suman Sarkar           | Dr. Malvika Sharma       | 10-Aug-2025 |

---

# Roles and Responsibilities of Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS) Members

### 1. Vigilance and Patrolling

The purpose of forming this squad is to maintain vigilance, oversight, and carry out patrolling functions across the campus.

Members of the Squad must remain:

* Mobile
* Alert
* Active at all times

The Anti-Ragging Squad shall:

* Conduct surprise raids in hostels and other locations vulnerable to ragging incidents.
* Inspect any location whenever suspicion arises.
* Carry out inspections whenever requested by the Dean (Student Affairs).

metadata.title: Appointment Of Anti-Ragging Squad (Ars) For Upcoming Semester

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Appointment of Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS) for Upcoming Semester.md_1
text:
### 2. Preliminary Inquiry into Ragging Complaints

The Anti-Ragging Squad (ARS) shall conduct an on-the-spot preliminary inquiry into any ragging incident referred by:

* Dean (Student Affairs)
* Parents of concerned students
* Students reporting such incidents

### 3. Collection of Evidence and Reporting

The Squad is empowered to:

* Collect incident reports
* Gather supporting documents
* Record facts and viewpoints related to the incident

The collected material shall be submitted to:

* Dean (Student Affairs)
* Anti-Ragging Committee (ARC)

for further inquiry and final action.

The Committee may also take immediate action under the Institute's Anti-Ragging Policy, subject to intimation and approval from the Dean (Student Affairs) / Anti-Ragging Committee.

### 4. Scheduled Surprise Visits

ARS members shall conduct surprise visits across the campus during:

**09:00 PM – 12:00 Midnight**

on their respective assigned duty dates.

### 5. Coordination with Institute Authorities

The Anti-Ragging Squad shall work in coordination with:

* Standing Institute-Level Anti-Ragging Committee (ARC)
* Dean (Student Affairs)

### 6. Transportation Support

ARS members may book a vehicle through **Saral (EG)** on their assigned duty date, if required.

### 7. Compliance with Anti-Ragging Regulations

The Anti-Ragging Squad is requested to strictly adhere to and effectively implement:

* Directives of the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India
* UGC Regulations on Curbing the Menace of Ragging in Higher Educational Institutions, 2009
* Subsequent amendments issued from time to time

---

## Issued By

**Assistant Registrar (Student Affairs)**

---

## Copy To

1. Director Office
2. Registrar Office
3. Dean (Academic Affairs)
4. Dean (Student Affairs)
5. Assistant Dean (Hostel Affairs)
6. Concerned Faculty Members
7. Council of Wardens
8. Security Office
9. Transportation Section – For arrangement of vehicles upon request through Saral by ARS members

---

*Digitally Signed by*
**Ram Prakash Prajapat**
*23 July 2025, 19:26:12 IST*

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination in Higher Education Institutions.md_0
text:
# Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination in Higher Education Institutions

## University Grants Commission (UGC)

**University Grants Commission**
(Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India)

Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg
New Delhi – 110002

**Phone:** 011-23236288 / 23239337
**Fax:** 011-23238858
**Email:** secy.ugc@nic.in

---

### From

**Prof. Rajnish Jain**
Secretary
University Grants Commission

**D.O. No.:** F.1-7/2011(SCT)
**Date:** 26 June 2019

---

# Subject: Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination in Higher Education Institutions

Respected Madam/Sir,

As you are aware, the University Grants Commission (UGC) is continuously monitoring the prevention of caste-based discrimination in Higher Educational Institutions.

The University Grants Commission has previously issued letters dated:

* 19.07.2011
* 02.07.2013
* 07.03.2016
* 05.09.2016
* 15.05.2017
* 04.06.2018

requesting institutions to take the following actions.

---

## Required Measures

### 1. Prevention of Discrimination

Officials and faculty members should refrain from any act of discrimination against SC/ST students on the basis of their social origin.

### 2. Complaint Registration Mechanism

Universities, institutes, and colleges should:

* Develop a dedicated webpage for lodging complaints of caste-based discrimination by SC/ST/OBC students.
* Maintain a complaint register in the Registrar's or Principal's Office.

If any such incident comes to the notice of the authorities, prompt action should be taken against the concerned official or faculty member.

### 3. Equal Treatment of Students

Universities and colleges should ensure that no official or faculty member indulges in any form of discrimination against any community or category of students.

### 4. Constitution of a Monitoring Committee

The University may constitute a committee to examine complaints of discrimination received from:

* SC students
* ST students
* OBC students
* Teachers
* Non-teaching staff

---

## Reporting Requirement

Institutions are requested to advise their officials and faculty members to remain sensitive while handling incidents of caste-based discrimination.

An **Action Taken Report (ATR)** in the prescribed format should be submitted to the UGC **within 30 days**.

### Submission Email

```text
sctsection@gmail.com
```

The instructions may also be circulated among all constituent and affiliated colleges of the University for necessary follow-up action.

---

With kind regards,

**(Rajnish Jain)**
Secretary
University Grants Commission

---

metadata.doc: Quick/Pdf-data/Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination in Higher Education Institutions.md
metadata.title: Prevention Of Caste-Based Discrimination In Higher Education Institutions

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination in Higher Education Institutions.md_1
text:
### Distribution

**The Vice-Chancellor of all Universities**

---

# Annexure

# Action Taken Report of Universities / Institutions to Prevent Caste-Based Discrimination

## Name of the University / Institution

---

---

## Reporting Format

| Sl. No. | Item                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1       | Whether the University/Institution has constituted any Committee to look into complaints of discrimination received from SC/ST/OBC students, teachers, and non-teaching staff. Provide details. |
| 2       | Whether the University/Institution has developed a webpage on its website for lodging complaints of caste-based discrimination by SC/ST/OBC students. Provide the URL and relevant details.     |
| 3       | Whether the University/Institution is maintaining a Complaint Register and the mode of action taken on complaints of caste-based discrimination.                                                |
| 3(i)    | Number of complaints received during the last one year.                                                                                                                                         |
| 3(ii)   | Number of complaints settled/resolved out of the total complaints received.                                                                                                                     |
| 3(iii)  | Whether any suicidal incidents or attempts related to such complaints were reported (provide numbers).                                                                                          |
| 3(iv)   | Action taken when incidents come to the notice of the authorities against the concerned official/faculty member.                                                                                |
| 4       | Whether the University/Institution has established any Cell to examine complaints of discrimination against SC/ST/OBC students received from affiliated or constituent colleges.                |
| 4(i)    | Mode adopted for resolving college-level grievances.                                                                                                                                            |
| 4(ii)   | Number of complaints received and resolved.                                                                                                                                                     |
| 4(iii)  | Type of instructions or directions issued to colleges regarding prevention of caste-based discrimination.                                                                                       |

---

# Objective

The purpose of this directive is to ensure that Higher Educational Institutions:

* Provide an environment free from caste-based discrimination.
* Establish accessible grievance redressal mechanisms.
* Take prompt corrective action against discriminatory conduct.
* Maintain transparency through complaint registers and reporting systems.
* Promote equal opportunity, dignity, and inclusion for all students and staff.

metadata.title: Prevention Of Caste-Based Discrimination In Higher Education Institutions

text:
# Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination

## University Grants Commission (UGC)

**University Grants Commission**
35, Feroze Shah Road
New Delhi – 110001

**Reference No.:** F.1-7/2011(SCT)
**Date:** 04 June 2018

---

### To

**The Registrar**
Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu
(Temporary Campus) Opposite Police Station
Paloura, Jammu – 181121

---

## Subject: Prevention of Caste-Based Discrimination

Sir/Madam,

As you are aware, the University Grants Commission (UGC) is continuously monitoring the prevention of caste-based discrimination in higher educational institutions.

The University Grants Commission has issued letters dated:

* 19.07.2011
* 02.07.2013
* 01.03.2016
* 05.09.2016
* 15.05.2017

requesting institutions to take the following actions:

### 1. Prevention of Discrimination

Officials and faculty members should desist from any act of discrimination against SC/ST students on grounds of their social origin.

### 2. Complaint Redressal Mechanism

Universities, institutes, and colleges may:

* Develop a dedicated webpage for lodging complaints regarding caste discrimination by SC/ST students.
* Maintain a complaint register in the office of the Registrar/Principal.

If any such incident comes to the notice of the authorities, prompt action should be taken against the concerned official or faculty member.

### 3. Equal Treatment of Students

Universities and colleges should ensure that no official or faculty member indulges in any form of discrimination against any community or category of students.

### 4. Constitution of a Monitoring Committee

The University should constitute a committee to examine complaints relating to discrimination received from:

* SC students
* ST students
* OBC students
* Teachers
* Non-teaching staff

---

## Additional Directions

Officials and faculty members should be advised to exercise greater sensitivity while dealing with incidents related to caste discrimination.

Institutions are requested to submit an **Action Taken Report (ATR)** to the UGC in the prescribed format during the academic year 2017–18.

**Email for submission:**

```text
sctsection@gmail.com
```

The above instructions may also be circulated among all constituent and affiliated colleges of the University for necessary follow-up action.

---

**Yours faithfully,**

**(Madhu Mehra)**
*Under Secretary*
University Grants Commission

---

# Annexure

## Action Taken Report of Universities/Institutions to Prevent Caste-Based Discrimination (2017–18)

### Name of the University

---

metadata.title: Prevention Of Caste-Based Discrimination

text:
### Information to be Submitted

| Category | Website Address for Lodging Complaints of Caste-Based Discrimination by SC/ST/OBC Students, Teachers & Non-Teaching Staff | Number of Complaints Received During 2017–18 | Number of Complaints Disposed Of | Whether Any Corrective Action Was Taken Against the Delinquent Official/Faculty | Whether University Has Constituted a Committee to Deal with Such Complaints | Mode of Constitution of Committee | Number of Meetings Held | Action Taken Against Delinquent Official/Faculty |
| -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| SC       |                                                                                                                           |                                              |                                  |                                                                                 |                                                                             |                                   |                         |                                                  |
| ST       |                                                                                                                           |                                              |                                  |                                                                                 |                                                                             |                                   |                         |                                                  |
| OBC      |                                                                                                                           |                                              |                                  |                                                                                 |                                                                             |                                   |                         |                                                  |

---

### Declaration

If the University has **not constituted a committee**, the reason for non-constitution should be clearly specified.

---

## Purpose of this Circular

The UGC emphasizes that:

* Educational institutions must remain free from caste-based discrimination.
* Equal opportunity and dignity must be ensured for all students and staff.
* Effective grievance redressal mechanisms should be established.
* Institutions must maintain accountability through regular reporting and monitoring.

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md_0
text:
# Some Points to Remember About Ragging

## 1. What Constitutes Ragging?

Ragging is an act of aggression committed by an individual or a group of individuals against another individual or group. The aggressors derive authority merely from being senior to the victims, while the victims become targets simply because they are new to the institution.

> Any interaction that is **aggressive**, **intimidating**, or **asymmetric in power dynamics** (not on equal footing) constitutes ragging.

---

## 2. Ragging Is Not Harmless Fun

In an institution such as IIT Jammu, students come from diverse social, cultural, linguistic, and economic backgrounds. An act that may appear harmless or humorous to one person can cause humiliation, distress, or psychological harm to another.

There have been numerous incidents across the country where ragging has resulted in:

* Severe emotional trauma
* Loss of self-esteem
* Depression and anxiety
* Suicides
* Fatalities

Often, senior students do not realize when an interaction crosses acceptable boundaries. The potential harm far outweighs any perceived benefits.

---

## 3. Ragging Does Not Build Confidence or Smartness

A common misconception is that ragging makes freshers bold, confident, or socially adept.

In reality, ragging:

* Forces individuals into submission
* Encourages conformity rather than individuality
* Suppresses creativity and independent thinking
* Reduces self-worth and confidence
* Discourages innovation and free expression

Attempts to humiliate newcomers often create feelings of bitterness and resentment, which can perpetuate a cycle where victims later become perpetrators.

---

## 4. Ragging Does Not Help Discover Talent

Human talent is diverse, complex, and multi-dimensional.

No individual has the authority to determine:

* What another person's talents are
* Whether someone needs to be "brought out of their shell"
* How another person should behave socially

It is unfair and regressive to make students feel untalented, inadequate, or socially inferior.

---

## 5. Academic Performance Suffers

The academic structure at IIT Jammu is demanding.

As a general guideline:

> For every credit registered, an average student is expected to devote approximately one hour of study or academic work.

When freshers are deprived of valuable time because of ragging or unwanted interactions, it can lead to:

* Poor academic performance
* Increased stress
* Difficulty adapting to institute life

---

metadata.doc: Quick/Pdf-data/Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md
metadata.title: Some Points To Remember About Ragging

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md_1
text:
## 6. Freshers Deserve the Freedom of Self-Discovery

A premier institution like IIT Jammu offers numerous opportunities for students to excel in:

* Academics
* Research
* Innovation
* Sports
* Cultural activities
* Leadership roles
* Entrepreneurship

Imposing predetermined beliefs, behaviors, or expectations on freshers restricts their ability to discover their own interests, strengths, and identities.

---

## 7. Ragging Harms the Reputation of the Nation

It is a matter of concern that searches for the term **"ragging"** frequently highlight incidents from India.

In many countries, ragging has largely disappeared from educational institutions without reducing:

* Institutional pride
* Student bonding
* Alumni engagement
* Sense of belonging

Healthy interaction and mentorship can thrive without ragging.

---

## 8. Eliminating Ragging Is a Collective Responsibility

Ragging cannot be eradicated solely through strict rules, disciplinary measures, or punishments.

Long-term change requires:

* Awareness
* Empathy
* Responsibility
* Active participation from students

Every student should:

* Refrain from ragging others
* Intervene when witnessing ragging
* Support affected students
* Promote a culture of respect and inclusion

---

## Final Appeal

Ragging is a social evil that undermines the values of respect, equality, and personal dignity.

> Every student has a responsibility not only to avoid ragging but also to actively prevent it.

Let us work together to create a campus environment that is welcoming, inclusive, and conducive to learning, growth, and mutual respect.

metadata.doc: Quick/Pdf-data/Some Points to Remember About Ragging.md
metadata.title: Some Points To Remember About Ragging

# Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

**Jagti, NH-44, Nagrota, Jammu (Jammu & Kashmir) – 181221**
**Email:** registrar@iitjammu.ac.in

---

**Notification No.: 42 of 2026**
**Reference No.: IIT Jmu/Reg./2026/93**
**Date:** 15 June 2026

# Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC)

## References

1. DO No. F.1-13/2022 (CPP-II) dated 19.09.2024 and IITJMU/SW/11-17/2/2024/930 dated 24 September 2024.
2. IITJMU/SW/11-17/2/2025/286 dated 13 October 2025.

---

## Constitution of the Student Grievance Redressal Committee

In compliance with the Gazette Notification dated **11 April 2023**, and superseding earlier notifications issued under the references cited above, the Competent Authority is pleased to constitute the **Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC)** at IIT Jammu as follows:

| S. No. | Name / Designation            | Role            | Remarks                      |
| ------ | ----------------------------- | --------------- | ---------------------------- |
| 1      | Dean (Faculty Affairs)        | Chairperson     | Ex-officio                   |
| 2      | Dean (Administrative Affairs) | Member          | Ex-officio                   |
| 3      | Dean (Academic Programs)      | Member          | Ex-officio                   |
| 4      | Dean (Student Affairs)        | Member          | Ex-officio                   |
| 5      | Prof. Meenakshi Rajeev        | Member          | —                            |
| 6      | Liaison Officer for SC/ST     | Member*         | Ex-officio                   |
| 7      | Liaison Officer for OBC       | Member*         | Ex-officio                   |
| 8      | Liaison Officer for PwD       | Member*         | Ex-officio                   |
| 9      | General Secretary (Students)  | Special Invitee | Ex-officio                   |
| 10     | HoS (Academic Programs)       | Convener        | For all academic matters     |
| 11     | HoS (Student Affairs)         | Convener        | For all non-academic matters |

---

### Note

> *Members marked with an asterisk (*) shall be invited depending upon the nature of the grievance under consideration.*

---

## Tenure of the Committee

The Student Grievance Redressal Committee shall remain in force for a period of **two years**:

**15 June 2026 – 31 May 2028**

---

## Appointment of Ombudsperson

The Institute has nominated:

### Dr. S. N. Singh

**Emeritus Professor, IIT Delhi**

as the **Ombudsperson** for a period of **three years**:

**15 June 2026 – 31 May 2029**

The Ombudsperson shall act in accordance with the applicable regulations governing student grievance redressal.

---

## Language Clarification

This notification has been issued in both **English** and **Hindi**.

> In the event of any ambiguity, inconsistency, or difference in interpretation between the two versions, the **English version shall be treated as final and authoritative**.

---

metadata.title: Student Grievance Redressal Committee

text:
## Issued By

**(Col. Virinder Singh Jeji (Retd.))**
**Registrar**
Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

---

## Distribution

### Copy To

1. Director's Office
2. Webmaster (for uploading on the Institute website)

---

## Purpose of the SGRC

The Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC) serves as the formal institutional mechanism for addressing and resolving grievances raised by students concerning academic and non-academic matters. The committee functions in accordance with UGC regulations and Institute policies to ensure fairness, transparency, and timely redressal of student concerns.

id: chunk_Quick_Pdf-data_Zero-Tolerance for Ragging in the Institute.md_1
text:
### Subject: Zero-Tolerance for Ragging in the Institute

Dear Parents,

As you know, with the upcoming academic year, IIT Jammu will be welcoming a fresh batch of students who have qualified the JEE (Advanced). Being an Institute of National Importance, IIT Jammu is pleased to bring in this new batch of bright and talented students.

Every year, IIT Jammu puts in great effort to ensure that the new entrants are well adjusted to their new environment. This effort is put in by the faculty and students alike by organising events such as the Foundation Program, Cultural Fest, student-led orientations, and many other extracurricular and co-curricular activities which ensure that the students develop a sense of self-confidence and team spirit.

As you may be well aware, the senior batches play a vital role in creating a welcoming and amicable environment for the new batch. We are sure that your ward, who is one of the senior students, will put in the best of efforts to play a constructive role for the junior batch. However, a small minority of students end up indulging in unethical interactions with the juniors, and that leaves a negative impression on them. Therefore, it is the Institute's fundamental responsibility to stringently oppose ragging and similar activities which discourage the growth and expressive capabilities of the new batch.

> **IIT Jammu upholds the policy of "zero tolerance" towards ragging. Any student found ragging another student will be summarily dismissed.**

In this respect, the Hon'ble Supreme Court of India has issued directives to curb the menace of ragging in educational institutions. For more details regarding the Institute's anti-ragging stance, please visit the anti-ragging portal:

**http://iitjammu.ac.in/anti-ragging**

The prevailing laws ban ragging in any form in the country, and any violation of this is punishable by:

* A fine of **₹25,000**
* **Expulsion from the Institute and the hostel**
* **Rigorous imprisonment of up to 3 years**

While the Institute encourages positive interactive activities amongst the first-year and senior students of the hostels, indiscipline of any form is viewed very seriously.

I would, therefore, urge you to advise your ward to contribute to constructing a nurturing environment in the Institute and the hostels, and discourage any form of indiscipline or ragging that they witness. Furthermore, your ward may please be advised in no uncertain terms not to indulge in any form of indiscipline or ragging.

## Anti-Ragging

Ragging at IIT Campus is strictly prohibited and is a punishable offense as per institute honor code.

There are strict guidelines from the Hon'ble Supreme Court to prevent ragging at the institute campuses.

Please report to Hostel Management and Dean of Students about any such incident.

Some points to remember about ragging

Notification regarding Anti-Ragging Committee dated 17.08.2021

Notification regarding Anti-Ragging Committee dated 10.10.2022

Anti Ragging Squad-2024

Anti Ragging Details (ARC & ARS) - 2024

Director's Message on Anti-Ragging

Anti Ragging Squad-2025

Anti Ragging Details (ARC & ARS)-2025

## Prevention of caste-based discrimination

The officials, faculty and staff members should desist from any act of discrimination against SC/ST students on the grounds of their social origin.

Further, all stakeholders should be more sensitive while dealing with such incidents of caste discrimination.

Please report to below liaison officers about any such incident.

## Liaison Officers

| Name | Designation |
| --- | --- |
| Dr. Rimen Jamatia | Liaison Officer for SC/ST at IIT Jammu |
| Dr. Shiva S | Liaison Officer for OBC at IIT Jammu |
| Dr. Aditya Shankar Sandupatla | Liaison Officer for PwD at IIT Jammu |

text: Some points to remember about ragging • Ragging is an act of aggression committed by an individual or a group of individuals over another individual or a group of individuals where the first group, by virtue of their being senior to the second group, somehow get the authority and audacity to commit the act and the second group, by virtue of their being new to the institution are automatic victims. Any interaction which is aggressive and asymmetric (not on equal footing) is ragging. • Ragging is not harmless fun. In an institution like IIT Jammu students come from diverse social and cultural backgrounds, and what may be harmless fun for some may turn out be serious indignity for some others. There are always a few cases where ragging (or interaction) is intolerable and the fresher suffer loss of self-esteem. Nation-wide there have been many ragging deaths and suicides. It is often the case that seniors, being young students themselves, do not realize where to draw the line and when to back off – the risk is simply not worth it. • Ragging does not increase smartness or make a fresher bold. Quite to the contrary it forces an individual to accept subjugation and confirm; perhaps even rationalize it later (with the drinks and samosas at the end of the interaction period). This loss of individuality suppresses talent and reduces self-worth. In turn, the forced homogeneity precludes free flow of new ideas, creativity and the spirit of enquiry. Also, the attempt of the seniors to break the self-esteem of a newcomer and revel in their shame and fear engenders feelings of bitterness and revengefulness, which is often taken out on juniors next year. • Ragging does not help bring people out of their shells and bring out their talents. Talent in human beings is varied and multi-faceted. Identifying and recognizing talent is difficult business and one person cannot unilaterally decide that another needs “bringing out". It is also regressive to make some people feel that they are talent-less or that they lack social skills. • The courses in IIT Jammu are designed so that for every credit registered for an average student requires to put in an hour of study/work. Denying the freshers, the time to put in this work often results in poor academic performance. • A premier institution like IIT Jammu provides many opportunities for a new comer to

metadata.title: Anti-Ragging Homepage Menus Some-Pts.Pdf

text: another needs “bringing out". It is also regressive to make some people feel that they are talent-less or that they lack social skills. • The courses in IIT Jammu are designed so that for every credit registered for an average student requires to put in an hour of study/work. Denying the freshers, the time to put in this work often results in poor academic performance. • A premier institution like IIT Jammu provides many opportunities for a new comer to excel, in both academics and extra-curricular activities. Forcing a predetermined set of ideas on to a fresher diminishes his/her chances of self-discovery. • It is a matter of shame that a “Google search" on “ragging" returns most of the top few items related to ragging in India. Ragging, in most other countries, have become a thing of the past, and this has in no way reduced the sense of belonging to their institutions. • It is neither possible nor desirable that a high-handed discipline and punishments be used to stop ragging. If this social evil has to be eradicated it must happen through the understanding and efforts of the student body. Not only should they not rag others but should actually prevent ragging from happening. We make an earnest request to all students to play an active role in stopping ragging.
